title: 2 Terminology
body_text:
2	Terminology
2.1	Definitions
ACCULINK
Â®
ACCULINK is the brand name given to the automated shared network that provides debit card financial services at ATM and in-branch terminals between CCUA members (credit unions and caisses populaires), surcharge-free. All credit unions participate in the ACCULINK network. Many credit unions also participate in THE EXCHANGE
Â®
Network.
Acquirer
The acquirer is the financial institution that owns or sponsors the ATM used for the ATM transaction.
Allpoint
Allpoint is a US network with ATMs internationally that provide surcharge-free cash access for participating financial institutions and card providers.
ATM
An Automated Teller Machine (ATM) is an unattended PIN-entry terminal that allows cardholders to access basic banking services such as making deposits, withdrawing cash, or changing their Personal Identification Number (PIN).
BIN
A BIN is the bank identification number assigned to a financial institution. In Canada, the numbers are assigned and maintained by Payments Canada. The BIN can also be referred to as the International Identification Number (IIN).
C2C Node
The C2C Node is the hardware and software environment that facilitates the
Interac
Â®
Inter-Member Network (IMN) managed by Central 1 and operated by Cardtronics.
Cash Back
Cash back is an option available to customers to add an extra amount to a purchase during a POS debit transaction. The customer receives the added amount in cash.
CCUA
Canadian Credit Union Association is the national credit union trade association that provides services to Canadaâs credit unions, caisses populaires (outside of Quebec), and regional Central organizations.
Client Centre
The Client Centre is a digital portal that provides secured access to Central 1âs applications and to information and news about our products and services.
CNP
Card Not Present (CNP) refers to transactions for online purchases made with a co-badged (debitâcredit) card.
Confirmed Facsimile
A confirmed facsimile is a printout from the senderâs fax machine confirming the successful transmission of an outgoing fax which includes the recipientâs fax number.
Cooperative Node
The Cooperative Node is the hardware and software environment that facilitates the
Interac
InterâMember Network (IMN) managed by CCUA and operated by Everlink.
CPA
CPA means Canadian Payments Association, also known as Payments Canada.
Debit Card
A debit card is an electronic transaction card that enables consumers to access their account(s) via ATMs and POS terminals when used in conjunction with a PIN.
Designated Settlement Central
A designated settlement central is the central that is selected to process settlement for other
Interac
financial institutions and offset settlement and reconciliation to participating financial institutions. Central 1 is the designated settlement central for financial institutions under the C2C Node. CUPS is the designated settlement central for financial institutions under the Cooperative Node.
Direct Connector
A direct connector is a financial institution processing a switch or banking system that communicates directly with the Cooperative Node.
EXCHANGE Network
THE EXCHANGE is a network of credit unions and banks that have agreed to allow cardholders access to their shared ATMs with no surcharges. This gives cardholders free access to thousands of ATMs across Canada and in the US via the Allpoint network.
Exception Items
Exception items are items deposited at an ATM that canât be processed as part of a Canadian dollar deposit (e.g., foreign currency cheques, foreign cash, utility payments).
IIN
An IIN is the Institution Identification Number assigned to a financial institution. The IIN can also be referred to as the Bank Identification Number (BIN).
Interac
Cash
Interac
Cash is a service offered by
Interac
that allows cardholders to obtain cash at any ATM that displays the
Interac
logo.
Interac
Debit
Interac
Debit is a service that allows cardholders to make purchases at merchant locations. Transactions are approved online and in real time as the money is exchanged electronically between the financial institution and the merchant. See POS.
Interac
Member
An
Interac
member is a licensed participant in
Interac
services. There are currently four types of membership. Central 1 and CCUA are licensed members of
Interac
as Direct Connecting Financial Institutions (DCFI). A DCFI operates an Inter-Member Node (IMN).
IMN
The Inter-Member Node (IMN) is the common software environment licensed by
Interac
for members to use. Each Direct Connector member operates an IMN.
Issuer
The issuer is the financial institution that issued the card used for the ATM transaction.
PAN
A PAN is the primary account number. This number is used in ATM transactions to identify the financial institution and the card number or account number of the cardholder.
POS
A Point-of-Sale (POS) is the retailer terminal through which automated debit or credit transactions are entered. See
Interac
Debit.
Purchase
A purchase is an electronic debit to a cardholderâs account, transacted through a POS terminal, in payment for goods and/or services purchased from a retailer.
Refund
A refund is an electronic credit to a cardholderâs account, originated through a POS terminal by a retailer, to reimburse a cardholder for unused or returned goods or services.
Retailer
A retailer is a merchant who has entered into an agreement with a retailer financial institution to offer consumers the option of paying for goods or services using POS.
Retailer Financial Institution
A retailer financial institution is the financial institution that supplies a retailer with access to the POS system.
Reversal
A reversal is a transaction that reverses a POS purchase transaction. A reversal can be processed when a cardholder cancels a purchase transaction, when a card issuer determines that a transaction isnât valid, or when a merchant reverses an incorrectly entered transaction.
Switch
A switch is the software function performed by a supplier, which receives and generates transaction messages sent from one organization to another organization participating in a network or payment scheme. The switch determines the proper destination/routing for each message and transmits the message to the destination.
END
